title_zh: 德尼斯·格雷夫斯的第二幕
rubric_en: Denyce Graves is retiring from performing after a career as one of opera’s leading women. But there’s more work for her to do.
rubric_zh: 德尼斯·格雷夫斯将结束舞台演出，在走完歌剧界最顶尖女高音的职业生涯后，转身而去。但她仍有更多工作要做。

paragraphs.en: When the curtain of New York’s Metropolitan Opera House rose for the closing matinee of Porgy and Bess in January, the boos that typically accompany the entrance of the show’s villains were a mere murmur. The nearly 4,000 people who packed the space to capacity—175 of them standing-room ticket holders who remained on their feet for the opera’s three-and-a-half-hour run time—had come to cheer.
paragraphs.zh: 当纽约大都会歌剧院的幕布为《波吉与贝丝》一场1月的闭幕日午场拉开时，通常会在剧中反派登场时响起的嘘声只剩下一阵轻微的低语。那晚几乎坐满了近4000名观众——其中175人是站票持有人，在这部时长三个半小时的歌剧里一直站立；他们来这里只为为她喝彩。

paragraphs.en: Thirty-one years before, Denyce Graves had made her Met debut in the title role of Georges Bizet’s Carmen. The mezzo-soprano had been a revelation, her full, rich voice and lusty physicality defining the role for a generation. Graves was a diva in the original, operatic sense: a world-renowned performer who made journalists wilt, and whose name alone was enough to draw crowds. But here she was, playing a supporting character in Porgy and Bess. Graves was singing the part of Maria, the matriarch of the 1920s working-class Black community of Catfish Row, the Lowcountry settlement where the show takes place. It was set to be her final performance ever, a return to the opera that had launched her professional career in 1985.
paragraphs.zh: 31年前，德尼斯·格雷夫斯就以歌剧《卡门》中的同名主角在大都会歌剧院完成了处女登场。作为一位中音女高音，她的出现令人耳目一新，浑厚丰盈的嗓音与强势的舞台表现力为一代人重新定义了这个角色。格雷夫斯就是“歌剧意义上的女神”：一位享誉世界的表演者，能让记者都不敢轻易发言，她的名字本身就能吸引观众入场。但在这里，她只是在《波吉与贝丝》中饰演配角。格雷夫斯演的是玛丽娅，扮演卡特菲什街上20世纪20年代工人阶层黑人社群的族长。她本次演出原本是她的告别演出，回到了1985年开启职业生涯的这部歌剧。

paragraphs.en: After intermission, but before the opera resumed, the entire company crowded onto the stage, and the house rose to its feet. Peter Gelb, the company’s general manager, presented Graves with a plaque recognizing her career. It would be installed in the Met’s List Hall, where aspiring artists audition. “My heart is unrehearsed at having to hold so much love,” Graves said, tearing up and taking a few beats to collect herself. “It has never been asked to hold this capacity of love before.”
paragraphs.zh: 休息过后、演出继续前，整个剧团都走上舞台，观众也齐刷刷站了起来。该剧团的总经理彼得·盖尔布向格雷夫斯颁发了一块纪念牌，以表彰她的职业生涯，并将其挂在大都会歌剧院的李斯特大厅（List Hall），也就是有志艺人来这里试镜的地方。格雷夫斯哽咽着说：“我完全没有预料到自己会承受如此深厚的爱，我从未被要求承载过这种分量。”

paragraphs.en: It was a rare moment of harmony in a year—for opera as for much else—that had been defined by conflict. Just weeks after his second inauguration, President Trump had fired members of the board of trustees at the Kennedy Center—the longtime home of the Washington National Opera, the other major opera company that Graves had performed with for decades. He handpicked the artists recognized for the Kennedy Center Honors, banned drag queens from performing there, and affixed his name to the building’s facade. He successfully pushed to dismantle the Corporation for Public Broadcasting, which for decades had helped opera find audiences on television and radio. After the WNO voted to leave the Kennedy Center early this January, and after several acts refused to play the venue in protest of Trump’s changes, he announced that the building would be closed for “renovations” for two years.
paragraphs.zh: 这是一年里罕见的和解时刻——不只在歌剧界，而在更广泛的领域同样如此；这一年几乎被冲突定义。特朗普第二次就职仅数周后，便解除了肯尼迪中心的董事会成员职务——这是华盛顿国家歌剧院的长期驻地，也是格雷夫斯演唱了数十年的另一家大型歌剧公司。随后他指定了肯尼迪中心荣誉奖的提名艺术家，禁止变装皇后在该场馆演出，并把自己的名字贴在大楼外立面上。他还成功推动解散公共广播公司这一机构，而该机构几十年来一直帮助歌剧在电视和广播上走进更多观众视野。1月初，在WNO投票决定提前离开肯尼迪中心，且多位歌手组织拒演以抗议特朗普改革后，他宣布该建筑将因“改造”闭馆两年。

paragraphs.en: Read: What I saw inside the Kennedy Center
paragraphs.zh: 阅读：《我在肯尼迪中心里所见》

paragraphs.en: The administration had also engaged in a sweeping campaign against Black history—against what it calls “wokeness”—perhaps most notably demanding a review of every exhibit in the Smithsonian’s halls, singling out the National Museum of African American History and Culture, with its unflinching portrayal of slavery, as a purveyor of “corrosive ideology.” Two days before Porgy and Bess’s closing matinee, National Park Service workers removed an exhibit at Philadelphia’s Independence National Historical Park memorializing nine people enslaved by George Washington (the administration is now appealing a federal judge’s order that the exhibit be restored).
paragraphs.zh: 该政府还对黑人历史发起了全面攻势——它所谓的“政治正确”反击，其中最引人注目的是要求对史密森学会大厅中的每一件展品进行审查，点名批评国家非裔美国人历史与文化博物馆对奴隶制问题的直白呈现，称其传播“腐蚀性意识形态”。在《波吉与贝丝》最后一场下午场演出前两天，国家公园管理局工作人员移除了费城独立国家历史公园中一处纪念品——纪念乔治·华盛顿所奴役的九个人的展览；该政府如今正上诉联邦法官要求恢复该展览的裁定。

paragraphs.en: Graves’s final performance brought her back to Catfish Row in Porgy and Bess, the opera that launched her career. (Courtesy of Richard Termine / Met Opera)
paragraphs.zh: 格雷夫斯的最后一场演出把她带回了《波吉与贝丝》中的卡特希夫街区，那部开创了她事业的歌剧。（图片来源：Richard Termine / 大都会歌剧院）

paragraphs.en: Graves is a perfect avatar of everything the Trump administration seeks to eradicate, a fact that gave her swan song an even more sentimental air. She has consistently used the artistic capital she amassed through her mastery of the European canon to unearth and preserve Black history, and to promote productions that challenge Eurocentrism. She’s sought to diversify the world of opera. And Graves does not consider her work finished, even if she has now walked offstage for the last time as a performer. With both the arts and Black history under attack, she is entering a new phase of her career, one that may well be more consequential than the first.
paragraphs.zh: 格雷夫斯完美地代表了特朗普政府想要清除的一切，而这也使她的谢幕更添几分感伤。她始终运用自己在欧洲歌剧经典曲目上积累的艺术资本，挖掘并保存黑人历史，同时推动那些挑战欧洲中心主义的作品。她一直在努力让歌剧界更具多元化。即便她已经作为表演者走下舞台，格雷夫斯也不认为自己的工作已经结束。面对艺术与黑人历史遭受双重打击，她正在开启职业生涯的新阶段，这一阶段甚至可能比第一阶段更具历史意义。

paragraphs.en: After the show, I found Graves backstage, already changed out of Maria’s plain apron, shift dress, and sensible black Mary Janes. She wore a strapless burgundy A-line number paired with stiletto pumps, an ensemble more befitting a diva. A crowd had gathered outside the stage door of the opera house, and another in the front plaza of Lincoln Center, even as frigid winds cut through layers of clothing like X-Acto blades. A staffer coordinated a receiving line so that Graves could greet her frozen public before she was taken to her retirement party.
paragraphs.zh: 演出结束后，我在后台见到了 Graves，她已经换下了 Maria 那套朴素的围裙、便装连衣裙和实用的黑色玛丽珍鞋。她穿着一件无肩带的酒红色 A 字裙搭配细跟高跟鞋，打扮得更像一位真正的歌剧女王。剧院舞台门外已聚集了一群观众，林肯中心前广场也围了人，即使刺骨的寒风能把穿在身上的层层衣物切得像 X-Acto 刀片一样生疼。一名工作人员安排好了接见队列，让 Graves 在被接去参加退场（退休）派对前，能够同她的观众们一一见面致意。

paragraphs.en: “I was genuinely surprised,” she told me, speaking about the intermission ceremony, her voice deep-toned, like polished mahogany. “You know what I thought about? I thought about what we see happening right now, with our history being erased.” The placement of the plaque in the Met’s audition hall, where a new generation of Black artists would see it and perhaps be inspired, was what had touched her most.
paragraphs.zh: “我真的很惊讶，”她对我说起中场表彰仪式时，声音低沉而浑厚，像打磨过的红木。“你知道我当时在想什么吗？我想的是我们现在正在目睹的一幕，尤其是我们历史被抹去的现象。” 她最受触动的是奖牌在大都会歌剧院试镜大厅里的摆放位置；在那里，一代又一代的非裔艺术家都会看到它，也许会因此受到鼓舞。

paragraphs.en: Graves’s path to the stage was challenging, in no small part because of racism. She was born in Washington, D.C., in 1964 and raised in a poor neighborhood in the city’s Southeast quadrant. When she was 4 years old, riots erupted after the assassination of Martin Luther King Jr., and nearly 12,000 federal troops were deployed to the city.
paragraphs.zh: Graves 的登台之路并不轻松，其中很大一部分原因都与种族主义有关。她于 1964 年出生在华盛顿特区，在这座城市东南区的一个贫困社区长大。她 4 岁时，马丁·路德·金恩被暗杀后，城市一度爆发骚乱，几乎有 12,000 名联邦军队被部署到这座城市。

paragraphs.en: Graves grew up singing in church, initially at the behest of her mother, Dorothy Graves-Kenner, who had to coax her daughter past her shyness and toward some sense of authority behind the microphone. Judith Allen, her first music teacher, recognized that she had something worth nurturing, both in voice and in presence. Allen took a young Graves to rehearsals of D.C.’s All City Chorus at Constitution Hall. At age 13, when Graves heard a record of Leontyne Price singing Puccini arias, she was struck with a revelation: She needed to be an opera singer.
paragraphs.zh: Graves 在教会唱歌成长，最初是应母亲 Dorothy Graves-Kenner 的要求，母亲不得不把她从胆怯中慢慢引到麦克风前，让她学会用更有掌控力的姿态发声。她的第一位音乐老师 Judith Allen 发现她既有天赋嗓音，也有舞台表现力，值得培养。Allen 带着年轻的 Graves 去参加哥伦比亚特区宪法厅（Constitution Hall）D.C. All City Chorus 的排练。Graves 13 岁时，听到一张 Leontyne Price 演唱普契尼咏叹调的唱片，顿时恍然明白：她的人生方向应该是成为一名歌剧演员。

paragraphs.en: With Allen’s encouragement, Graves auditioned for the Duke Ellington School of the Arts, a D.C. public magnet high school seemingly worlds away from the racialized poverty that characterized her neighborhood. Still, when Graves informed her mother that she wanted to study singing in college, Graves-Kenner was surprised. People attended college to become doctors or lawyers. What on earth was her daughter going to do with a degree in something she’d already learned in church?
paragraphs.zh: 在艾伦的鼓励下，格雷夫斯参加了哥伦比亚特区公共磁吸高中——杜克·埃灵顿艺术学校的试镜，那所学校看起来与她所在社区因种族化贫困所定义的环境仿佛天壤之别。尽管如此，当格雷夫斯告诉母亲她想在大学主修声乐时，格雷夫斯-肯纳还是感到意外。人们读大学通常是为了成为医生或律师。她的女儿拿着一门在教堂里早已学过的“学位”，到底能做什么呢？

paragraphs.en: Graves ultimately studied at Oberlin College, where she encountered the first in a procession of gatekeepers who saw her as a misfit or a novelty because they believed that opera was a white art form. A professor told her, “This is not a place for you” when she showed up to his class, she recalls. Still, in 1985, she signed a contract with the Tulsa Opera, taking roles in Porgy and Bess and The Magic Flute. The former carries a fraught reputation because it is a work about poor Black people, written and composed in 1934–35 by a white creative team (George and Ira Gershwin and DuBose Heyward), and almost always conducted and directed by white leaders. Even now, aspiring Black opera singers are warned against Porgy and Bess, lest they find themselves confined to Catfish Row for the rest of their career.
paragraphs.zh: 格雷夫斯最终在奥伯林学院就读，在那里她遇到了第一批把守者，这些人把她视为异类或新奇之人，原因是他们认为歌剧是一门白人艺术。她回忆说，有一位教授在她走进课堂时对她说：“这儿不适合你。”尽管如此，1985年她还是与塔尔萨歌剧院签下合同，出演了《波吉与贝丝》和《魔笛》中的角色。《波吉与贝丝》长期以来备受争议，因为它讲述的是贫穷的黑人生活，由一支白人创作团队——乔治和艾拉·格什温以及杜博斯·海沃德——在1934年至1935年创作并作曲，而且几乎总是由白人指挥和导演执掌。至今，许多有志于发展的黑人歌剧演员仍被劝阻演《波吉与贝丝》，以免在整个职业生涯中被困在卡特菲什·罗（Catfish Row）。

paragraphs.en: But Graves, like her Black-diva predecessors—Price, Marian Anderson, Jessye Norman—has always had a gift for transforming domains in which she was considered foreign, and making them bend to her. She went on to perform with the Vienna State Opera, London’s Royal Opera, and the Paris Opera. Graves met her friend and close collaborator Francesca Zambello, now the WNO artistic director, when Graves was singing with the Bavarian State Opera and Zambello was directing the company’s production of Otello. In 1995, Graves made her Met debut in Carmen. A busload of 75 family members and friends traveled from Washington to see her.
paragraphs.zh: 但像她的前辈“黑人女高音”——普赖斯、玛丽安·安德森、杰茜·诺曼——一样，格雷夫斯一直拥有把自己原本被认为陌生的领域转化过来的天赋，并让这些领域向她低头。她后来在维也纳国家歌剧院、伦敦皇家歌剧院和巴黎歌剧院登台演出。格雷夫斯在巴伐利亚国家歌剧院演出时，与后来成为华盛顿国家歌剧院（WNO）艺术总监的挚友兼长期合作伙伴弗朗西斯卡·赞贝洛相识。当时，赞贝洛正在执导该团《奥赛罗》的制作。1995年，格雷夫斯在大都会歌剧院《卡门》中首次亮相。75名家人和朋友坐着一辆大巴从华盛顿专程赶来观看她的演出。

paragraphs.en: For most of Denyce Graves’s career, the titular character of Carmen was the role most people associated with her. (Kathryn Osler / The Denver Post / Getty)
paragraphs.zh: 在丹妮斯·格雷夫斯的大部分职业生涯里，卡门这个主角角色都是大多数人最先想到与她关联在一起的身份。 （凯瑟琳·奥斯勒 / 丹佛邮报 / 盖蒂）

paragraphs.en: She was incredibly magnetic. Before that Met debut, a smitten Morley Safer interviewed her in her dressing room for 60 Minutes, seeming more nervous to speak with her than she was about performing. Graves soon became an opera evangelist to young children, appearing multiple times on Sesame Street, including in a memorable segment where she uses Bizet’s “Habanera” melody to fashion a lullaby for Elmo. She sang with BeBe Winans and Patti LaBelle, and released several albums. But if you really wanted to witness her talent in its fullest, you had to go see her in her element.
paragraphs.zh: 她有着惊人的磁性魅力。在那次大都会歌剧院首秀之前，沉醉于她的《60分钟》采访记者莫利·萨弗在她的化妆间与她交谈时，似乎比她上台演出时还紧张。此后，格雷夫斯很快成为面向儿童的“歌剧布道者”，多次出现在《芝麻街》，其中一段令人难忘的节目中，她用比才《哈巴内拉》的旋律为艾摩姆（Elmo）改编了一段催眠曲。她与贝贝·威南斯（BeBe Winans）和帕蒂·拉贝尔（Patti LaBelle）合唱，并发行了几张专辑。但如果你真想看到她才华的全部风采，就必须到现场，在她最擅长的舞台环境中观看。

paragraphs.en: Unlike musical theater, there are no microphones in opera. When singing fills an opera house, it floats on the power of the lungs, assisted by only the acoustics and architecture of the room. Both the form and its fans can be unforgiving. But Graves was an experience, the sort of performer who reaches through the proscenium, grabs you, and doesn’t let go until curtain. She more than held her own when sharing the stage with the Three Tenors—Plácido Domingo, José Carreras, and Luciano Pavarotti—the supergroup that made opera sexy and popular in the ’90s.
paragraphs.zh: 与音乐剧不同，歌剧里没有麦克风。歌唱要充满整个歌剧院，完全依靠肺活量，再加上场馆的声学效果与建筑结构来传递。无论是这种艺术形式本身还是它的观众，都不太手软、毫不宽容。但格雷夫斯是位“会成事”的表演者——那种能够冲破舞台框架、抓住你，直到落幕后才放手的人。她在与“三大男高音”——普拉西多·多明戈、何塞·卡雷拉斯与卢恰诺·帕瓦罗蒂——同台时，凭实力毫不逊色于他们，这对在20世纪90年代让歌剧变得更具吸引力并走向大众的“超级组合”来说并非易事。

paragraphs.en: In Carmen, and in her other signature role of Dalila in Camille Saint-Saëns’s Samson et Dalila, she played seductresses with such believability and authority that many profiles raved over the carnality she projected. Her crossover appeal extended to the halls of power. Graves sang at the inauguration of President George W. Bush and became close friends with Ruth Bader Ginsburg, singing at the justice’s funeral in 2020.
paragraphs.zh: 在《卡门》中，以及她在圣桑《参孙与大利拉》（Samson et Dalila）中另一个代表性角色大利拉里，她饰演的女主角都充满了令人信服且具权威感的诱惑力，许多人物特写都曾大加赞赏她所展现出的肉体性张力。她的跨界吸引力也延伸到权力中心。格雷夫斯曾在乔治·W·布什总统就职典礼上献唱，并与露丝·巴德·金斯伯格法官成为密友，还在2020年这位法官的葬礼上为其唱歌。

paragraphs.en: Graves used the fame and influence that Carmen brought to nurture her passion for Black artistic history. In 2005, she starred in the opera Margaret Garner, composed by Richard Danielpour, with a libretto by Toni Morrison. Garner was a woman in antebellum Kentucky whose escape from slavery—and decision to kill her daughter rather than allow her to return to bondage—had inspired Morrison’s 1987 novel, Beloved.
paragraphs.zh: 格雷夫斯利用《卡门》带来的声望和影响力，培育了自己对黑人艺术史的热忱。2005年，她主演了由理查德·丹尼尔波尔（Richard Danielpour）作曲、托妮·莫里森（Toni Morrison）编写剧本的歌剧《玛格丽特·加纳》（Margaret Garner）。加纳是奴隶制时期肯塔基的一位女性，她逃离奴役的经历，以及后来为了不让女儿再次陷入奴役而决意杀害自己孩子的选择，启发了莫里森1987年的小说《宠儿》（Beloved）。

paragraphs.en: Margaret Garner brought Black artists together to an extent that few operas could, and since its debut, Graves has consistently supported Black vocalists, conductors, composers, directors, and librettists. In 2013, she sang in the composer (and frequent Spike Lee collaborator) Terence Blanchard’s first opera, Champion. In 2021, she went back to the site of her first paying gig, the Tulsa Opera, to sing in Greenwood Overcomes, a production memorializing the 1921 Tulsa Race Massacre.
paragraphs.zh: 《玛格丽特·加纳》把黑人艺术家团结在一起的程度，是其他许多歌剧难以企及的；自这部作品首演以来，格雷夫斯一直持续支持黑人歌唱家、指挥家、作曲家、导演和剧作家。2013年，她演唱了作曲家、也是斯派克·李常合作伙伴的泰伦斯·布兰查德的第一部歌剧《冠军》。2021年，她回到了自己首次拿到有偿演出机会的地方——塔尔萨歌剧院，演出《格林伍德崛起》，这是一部纪念1921年塔尔萨种族大屠杀的作品。

paragraphs.en: Graves has always chased a sense of workplace and artistic camaraderie, of homecoming. I recognized it in the Met dressing rooms when I visited her: Black singers casually trading stories about jobs, directors, and cities, able to let down their guard and enjoy one another’s company. For much of Graves’s career, such a scene was a rarity. Instead, there was a tremendous, lonely pressure to be perfect. She wants things to be different for her heirs, for the future Black standard-bearers of opera. Implicit in this desire is also the desire for opera to persist, not as a remnant of its old grandeur, but as an art form that has been elevated by accessibility.
paragraphs.zh: 格雷夫斯一直追求一种职业与艺术的同行情谊，也是一种回归的归属感。我在梅特罗波利坦歌剧院的后台见她时认同了这种氛围：黑人歌手们随意交流工作、导演和城市的话题，能够放下戒备，真诚享受彼此的陪伴。格雷夫斯职业生涯的大部分时间里，这样的景象都很少见，取而代之的是巨大的、孤独的“必须完美”压力。她希望她的后继者——未来的黑人民间与主流歌剧传承者——能够拥有不同的处境。其内在愿望也意味着一种期望，即歌剧得以延续，但不是作为旧时辉煌的残影，而是作为一种因可及性而被重新提升的艺术形式。

paragraphs.en: Graves sang at the funeral of her friend Ruth Bader Ginsburg in 2020. (Greg Nash / Getty)
paragraphs.zh: 2020年，格雷夫斯在其好友鲁斯·巴德·金斯伯格的葬礼上演唱。（Greg Nash / Getty）

paragraphs.en: As I spoke with Graves in the weeks before her final performance, she seemed ready, happy even, to say goodbye to performing. She could finally set aside the monastic life required to keep her voice in top shape. “I know I’m not going to have dairy,” she said, explaining a typical day to me. “I know I’m not going to have vinegar. I know I’m not going to have all of those things which cause acid reflux and which will play out in the quality of the sound. I’m even thinking about talking to you, and the cost of that. If you’re a violinist, you’re not playing the violin from the moment you wake up until the moment you go to bed. But you’re using your voice. It’s very, very different.”
paragraphs.zh: 在她最后一场演出前的几个星期里，我与格雷夫斯交谈时，她似乎已做好了，也很开心地做好了告别舞台的准备。她终于可以放下为了保持嗓音巅峰状态而必须遵守的几乎修道士般的生活规律了。“我知道我不会再喝牛奶了，”她对我说着讲述自己一天的作息，“我知道我不会再喝醋了。我知道我不会再碰那些会引起胃酸倒流、并影响嗓音品质的东西。我甚至在想要不要再和你通话，还要考虑一下这到底要花多少钱。一个小提琴手不是从起床那一刻到上床休息那一刻都在拉小提琴，但你却一直在用你的声音。那是完全不同的。”

paragraphs.en: Although Graves might be newly able to partake in cheeses and vinaigrettes, she is far from retired, and intends to dedicate more time to directing while continuing to teach and build her foundation, which aims to promote more diverse representation in the vocal arts. She made her directorial debut in May 2022 with a Minnesota Opera production of Carmen. Last year, she directed the world premiere of Loving v. Virginia, by the composer Damien Geter and the librettist Jessica Murphy Moo, which tells the story of Richard and Mildred Loving, the couple at the center of the 1967 Supreme Court case that ruled anti-miscegenation laws unconstitutional.
paragraphs.zh: 虽然格雷夫斯现在可能终于可以重新吃奶酪和油醋汁了，但她离“退休”还差得很远，并且打算把更多精力投入到导演工作，同时继续教学，并建立自己的基金会。这个基金会旨在推动声乐艺术中更具多样性的代表性。她于2022年5月在明尼苏达歌剧院执导《卡门》时，完成了自己的导演处女作。去年，她导演了由作曲家达米安·盖特（Damien Geter）与剧作家杰西卡·墨菲·穆（Jessica Murphy Moo）合作完成的《洛文诉弗吉尼亚案》（Loving v. Virginia）世界首演。该作品讲述了理查德与米尔德丽德·洛文（Richard and Mildred Loving）的故事——这对夫妇是1967年最高法院裁定反乱伦婚（反种族通婚）法案违宪案件的核心人物。

paragraphs.en: This March, six weeks after her retirement from performing, Graves was slated to direct the opening production of Washington National Opera’s 70th spring season, an expanded and reimagined version of Scott Joplin’s 1911 opera, Treemonisha, with which the famed Black ragtime composer had intended to create a Black style of opera, before his death in 1917. Though Joplin paid to have the piano-vocal score published, his original full orchestrations were lost. Graves’s restoration would feature new orchestrations and arias by Damien Sneed and a new libretto adapted by the playwright Kyle Bass.
paragraphs.zh: 今年3月，在停止舞台表演仅六周后，格雷夫斯被安排执导华盛顿国家歌剧院（Washington National Opera）70周年春季演季开幕剧目。该剧目是对斯科特·乔普林于1911年创作的歌剧《特里诺米莎》（Treemonisha）的扩展版与重构版，在这部作品中，这位著名的黑人拉格泰姆作曲家在1917年去世前本想开创一种黑人风格的歌剧。尽管乔普林曾出资出版钢琴声乐总谱，但他原本完整的乐队编配版本已遗失。格雷夫斯的复兴版本将加入达米安·斯尼德（Damien Sneed）的新配器与咏叹调，以及由剧作家凯尔·巴斯（Kyle Bass）改编的新本本剧本。

paragraphs.en: Treemonisha, which is set in the Texas wilderness in 1884 and tells the story of a Black woman trying to rid her community of the influence of conjurers and superstition, was originally scheduled to open at the Kennedy Center Opera House. But that was before all the unpleasantness began with Trump. The show would instead debut at George Washington University’s Lisner Auditorium. And if the production did not previously register as political, a revival of Black opera’s would-be foundational work by D.C.’s opera-in-exile just a few blocks away from the Kennedy Center certainly reads as a provocative statement now.
paragraphs.zh: 《特里诺米莎》改编版将故事设定在1884年的德克萨斯荒野，讲述一位黑人女性试图让自己的社群摆脱巫师和迷信影响的故事，原本计划在肯尼迪中心歌剧院首演。但那是特朗普相关的所有不愉快事件出现之前。演出最后改在乔治华盛顿大学利斯纳剧院（Lisner Auditorium）亮相。若说这部作品过去未必显得有政治意味，那么如今在肯尼迪中心仅数个街区之外，由华盛顿的“流亡歌剧团”复排这部本可算作黑人歌剧奠基性作品的作品，无疑更像是一次具有挑衅意味的公开表态。

paragraphs.en: On opening night, when Francesca Zambello and WNO’s general director, Timothy O’Leary, took the stage to introduce the production, they were greeted with a standing ovation filled with whoops and throaty roars, as though they were a couple of outlaws in formal wear.
paragraphs.zh: 在开演之夜，弗朗西斯卡·赞贝洛（Francesca Zambello）与华盛顿国家歌剧院（WNO）总监蒂莫西·奥利（Timothy O’Leary）走上舞台介绍演出时，观众给他们热烈的起立鼓掌，掌声中夹杂着欢呼和低沉的吼叫，仿佛这两位身着礼服的“亡命之徒”般登场。

paragraphs.en: “We deeply appreciate your understanding, your solidarity, and your belief in creative freedom,” O’Leary said. The opera opened with a banjo solo, and Sneed played Joplin’s score on an upright piano onstage.
paragraphs.zh: “我们衷心感谢你们的理解、团结与对创作自由的信任，”奥莱瑞（O’Leary）说道。该歌剧以一段班卓独奏开场，斯尼德（Sneed）在舞台上用立式钢琴演奏了吉普林（Joplin）的谱曲。

paragraphs.en: The performance could never be exactly what it would have been in its planned venue. Opera is a big, melodramatic medium, designed for capturing big, melodramatic emotions. Although Lisner is a perfectly serviceable auditorium, it lacks the high ceilings, deep stage, and general grandeur of the opera house. The Treemonisha set didn’t have the three-dimensional, full-scale production value and enormous cast size typical of opera productions. The usual ornate set pieces were more modest and two-dimensional, relying on a floral-filigree wrap that evoked the background of a Kehinde Wiley painting. Sitting in the auditorium, I felt those constraints.
paragraphs.zh: 演出结果无论如何都不可能与原计划场地中的版本完全一样。歌剧是一种宏大、富于戏剧性的艺术形式，设计来承载强烈而夸张的情感。虽然 Lisner 是一个完全可用的礼堂，但它缺少歌剧院那样的高挑天花板、深邃舞台和整体的宏伟感。Treemonisha 的布景也没有歌剧制作里常见的三维、全尺寸舞台效果和庞大的演员阵容。平常华丽的布景道具在这里更为简朴且偏二维，只能依赖一种花纹盘绕、繁复纺织般的包裹结构来营造氛围，仿佛呼应了凯欣德·威利（Kehinde Wiley）画作中的背景。坐在观众席上，我确实感受到了这些限制。

paragraphs.en: Even so, Treemonisha’s themes played to the times, and after the final number, in which the title character and the chorus repeat the refrain of “Marching onward, marching onward,” the auditorium erupted. Graves and Sneed joined the performers for the curtain call. And then the cast and the crowd joined together to sing to a surprised Graves. The debut was on March 7, her 62nd birthday.
paragraphs.zh: 即便如此，Treemonisha 的主题仍然贴合时代，最后一个段落中，片名角色与合唱团反复齐唱“Marching onward, marching onward”（前进吧、前进吧）时，礼堂爆发出热烈的欢呼。格雷夫斯（Graves）与斯尼德（Sneed）也在谢幕时走向舞台与演员汇合。随后演员与观众一同合唱，令惊讶的格雷夫斯成为焦点。首演发生在3月7日，那是她62岁生日。

paragraphs.en: This article appears in the June 2026 print edition with the headline “The Diva.”
paragraphs.zh: 本文刊登在2026年6月的纸质版中，副标题为“The Diva”。
